title: Bookmarks and History — Persistence
description: Add a BookmarkManager that uses Python's shelve module to persistently save and load bookmarked directories and command history. Users can bookmark frequently visited directories and recall recent commands.
test_code:
import tempfile, os
assert callable(BookmarkManager), 'BookmarkManager should be a callable class'
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
    db_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test_bm')
    bm = BookmarkManager(db_path)
    assert callable(getattr(bm, 'add_bookmark', None)), 'Must have add_bookmark method'
    assert callable(getattr(bm, 'list_bookmarks', None)), 'Must have list_bookmarks method'
    assert callable(getattr(bm, 'remove_bookmark', None)), 'Must have remove_bookmark method'
    assert callable(getattr(bm, 'add_history', None)), 'Must have add_history method'
    assert callable(getattr(bm, 'get_history', None)), 'Must have get_history method'
    bm.add_bookmark('projects', '/home/user/projects')
    bm.add_bookmark('docs', '/home/user/docs')
    bookmarks = bm.list_bookmarks()
    assert isinstance(bookmarks, dict), 'list_bookmarks should return a dict'
    assert bookmarks.get('projects') == '/home/user/projects', f"Expected '/home/user/projects', got {bookmarks.get('projects')}"
    assert bookmarks.get('docs') == '/home/user/docs', f"Expected '/home/user/docs', got {bookmarks.get('docs')}"
    removed = bm.remove_bookmark('docs')
    assert removed == True, 'remove_bookmark should return True for existing bookmark'
    assert 'docs' not in bm.list_bookmarks(), 'docs should be removed'
    not_found = bm.remove_bookmark('nonexistent')
    assert not_found == False, 'remove_bookmark should return False for missing bookmark'
    bm.add_history('list /tmp')
    bm.add_history('search *.py')
    history = bm.get_history()
    assert isinstance(history, list), 'get_history should return a list'
    assert len(history) == 2, f'History should have 2 entries, got {len(history)}'
assert 'shelve' in sys.modules or 'shelve' in dir(), 'shelve module should be imported'
acceptance_criteria: BookmarkManager class with add_bookmark, list_bookmarks, remove_bookmark methods
add_history and get_history for command history
Uses shelve module for persistent storage
Data persists across BookmarkManager instances with same db_path
hints: Open shelve: with shelve.open(self.db_path) as db: ...
Store bookmarks under a key: db['bookmarks'] = {...}
Store history under a key: db['history'] = [...]
Read with db.get('bookmarks', {}) to handle first use
Remember to write back the modified dict/list to shelve
